title: Sweet Seeds and Summer Treats
story: Every August 3, people across America celebrate National Watermelon Day by chomping into the juiciest fruit of summer! Families gather for watermelon-eating contests, seeing who can finish their slice fastest without using their hands. Kids love spitting the black seeds as far as they can, while adults carve watermelons into fancy fruit bowls shaped like baskets or even animals. Grocery stores pile up pyramids of green striped melons, and restaurants serve watermelon salads, smoothies, and popsicles. Some people post photos of their perfectly triangular watermelon slices on social media, showing off the bright pink inside. It's the perfect way to stay cool and celebrate summer's sweetest treasure!
activity_question: What do kids love to do with watermelon seeds during National Watermelon Day celebrations?
activity_choices: Spit them as far as they can
activity_answer: Spit them as far as they can
bonus_fact: A watermelon is actually 92% water, making it nature's perfect summer drink!

title: Go Nuts for National Grab Some Nuts Day!
story: Every August 3, people across the country celebrate National Grab Some Nuts Day by munching on their favorite nutty snacks! Whether you love crunchy peanuts, buttery cashews, or fancy almonds, this holiday gives everyone permission to go a little nuts with their snacking. Families visit grocery stores to stock up on mixed nuts, while others crack open fresh walnuts or pecans. Some people get creative by making trail mix or baking nutty cookies. It's the perfect excuse to enjoy nature's protein-packed treats and discover new varieties you've never tried before!
activity_question: What do people often make with nuts on this holiday?
bonus_fact: Nuts can stay fresh for months when stored in airtight containers!
